description:
You are inside a running Linux terminal session.  
  A JavaScript/Node project already exists at  
  /home/user/project  

  PRE-EXISTING STRUCTURE
  ├── /home/user/project/package.json          (contains "version": "1.2.3")
  ├── /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md          (see below)
  └── /home/user/project/                     (write-able; no other version files)

  GOAL  
  1. Bump the project’s semantic version from 1.2.3 to 1.2.4 (patch bump) in
     /home/user/project/package.json.  
  2. Add a properly-formatted entry for version 1.2.4 in
     /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md.  
  3. Create a one-line log file recording the bump:
        /home/user/project/version_bump.log

  DETAILED REQUIREMENTS  

  A. package.json  
     • The JSON must still be valid.  
     • The value of the "version" key must become exactly "1.2.4".  
     • No other changes are required.

  B. CHANGELOG.md  
     • The file already begins with a markdown H1 line “# Changelog”, then an
       “[Unreleased]” section.  
     • Insert a new section directly beneath the existing “## [Unreleased]”
       header with the following exact markdown template
       (replace YYYY-MM-DD with today’s date, ISO-8601 format; keep the empty
       line before the bullet list):

       ## [1.2.4] - YYYY-MM-DD
       ### Fixed
       - Bump patch version to fix minor bugs.

     • The automated test will check:  
       1. There is exactly one header line that matches
          “## [1.2.4] - YYYY-MM-DD”.  
       2. It appears before the previous “## [1.2.3] - …” section.  
       3. The bullet list line is present verbatim.

  C. version_bump.log  
     • Create the file /home/user/project/version_bump.log (it does not yet
       exist).  
     • It must contain exactly one line, with no leading/trailing spaces or
       extra blank lines, in this pattern:

       1.2.4 - YYYY-MM-DD

     • YYYY-MM-DD must match the same date used in CHANGELOG.md.

  HOW TO VERIFY (for your own check)  
  1. cat /home/user/project/package.json  ➜ verify "version": "1.2.4"  
  2. grep -A2 "\[1.2.4]" /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md  ➜ see template above  
  3. cat /home/user/project/version_bump.log  ➜ single correct line.

  HINTS  
  • Only 2-3 shell commands are needed (e.g., sed or npm version).  
  • Remember to stay inside /home/user/project while editing.  
  • Keep JSON formatting intact; do not add trailing commas.  
  • Use $(date +%F) to obtain today’s date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
truth:
<!-- Files as they exist BEFORE the agent runs -->
  Initial package.json at /home/user/project/package.json:
  {
    "name": "myapp",
    "version": "1.2.3",
    "description": "Sample app"
  }

  Initial /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md content:

  # Changelog

  All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

  ## [Unreleased]

  ### Added
  - Initial stub entries.

  ## [1.2.3] - 2023-08-01
  ### Added
  - Previous stuff.


  --------------------------------------------------------------------
  EXPECTED STATE AFTER CORRECT COMPLETION
  --------------------------------------------------------------------

  /home/user/project/package.json MUST contain the line:
      "version": "1.2.4"

  /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md MUST contain the new block (date D is the
  current UTC/local date when the agent runs; test harness provides it):

  ## [1.2.4] - D
  ### Fixed
  - Bump patch version to fix minor bugs.

  and this block must appear immediately after the “## [Unreleased]” header and
  before “## [1.2.3] - 2023-08-01”.

  /home/user/project/version_bump.log MUST exist and contain exactly one line:
      1.2.4 - D
  where D is the same date string used above.

  The automated tests will:
  1. Parse package.json and assert that the "version" field == "1.2.4".
  2. grep the changelog for the regex
        ^## \[1\.2\.4] - ([0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2})$
     and capture the date; then ensure bullet list is present.
  3. Confirm version_bump.log exists, is one line long, and the line equals
        1.2.4 - <captured-date>
